title: Blocks, ItemStacks & Materials
description: Learn how to work with Minecraft blocks, items, and materials, including placing, modifying, creating ItemStacks, and custom attributes.

lessons.title: Blocks Basics
lessons.content:
# Working with Blocks

Blocks are the basic building units in Minecraft.

## Example: Place a Block
```java
Location loc = player.getLocation();
loc.getBlock().setType(Material.DIAMOND_BLOCK);
```

## Logic
- getBlock() → returns the block at the location
- setType(Material) → changes the block type

## Edge Cases
- Overwriting blocks may remove existing structures
- Some blocks require correct placement (e.g., redstone components)

quiz.question: Method to get a block at a location?
Method to set a block type?

lessons.title: ItemStack Basics
lessons.content:
# Creating ItemStacks

ItemStacks represent items in inventories.

## Example: Give Player an Item
```java
ItemStack diamond = new ItemStack(Material.DIAMOND, 5);
player.getInventory().addItem(diamond);
```

## Logic
- ItemStack(material, amount) creates item
- addItem() adds to inventory

## Edge Cases
- Inventory full → item may drop on ground

lessons.title: Custom Item Names & Lore
Item Enchantments & Flags
Understanding Materials
lessons.content:
# Materials Overview

Materials define the type of block or item.

## Example
```java
Material mat = Material.DIAMOND_BLOCK;
Material mat2 = Material.OAK_PLANKS;
```

## Logic
- Use Material enum
- Matches any block/item type

## Edge Cases
- Some materials deprecated
- Not all materials usable in certain versions

lessons.title: Block Interactions & Events
Advanced ItemStack Techniques
lessons.content:
# Advanced Techniques

## Cloning Items
```java
ItemStack original = new ItemStack(Material.DIAMOND_SWORD);
ItemStack clone = original.clone();
```

## Checking Item Type
```java
if (item.getType() == Material.DIAMOND_SWORD) {}
```

## Modifying Amount
```java
item.setAmount(10);
```

## Logic
- clone() → copies ItemStack
- getType() → check item
- setAmount() → change stack size

## Edge Cases
- Modifying immutable ItemStacks may cause unexpected behavior
